name: Tea (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Tea (Camellia sinensis) [Teas] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DTU, DUKE, PHENOL EXPLORER, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FDC 171917 (Beverages, tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water, SR Legacy)
Yu, Yang et al. 2023, Molecules 28:2339 doi:10.3390/molecules28052339 (organic acids of tea review)
Zhang et al. 2022, Food Science & Nutrition doi:10.1002/fsn3.2823 (organic acids sour taste black vs green tea)
Chandini/Rajalakshmi et al. 2015, PMC4520228 (extraction kinetics: gallic acid 8 mg/g, EGCG 11.8 mg/g, caffeine 42 mg/g leaf)
Keenan et al. 2011, Food Chemistry doi:10.1016/j.foodchem.2010.10.049 (theanine ~24 mg/200 mL cup)
USDA ARS jfca_teafluoride / Malinowska (fluoride 373+/-49 ug/100 g brewed black tea)
provenance.curation_notes: Interpreted as unsweetened brewed BLACK TEA INFUSION (~100 mL), the form a microbe would grow in; it is an extremely dilute, nutrient-poor medium (~99.7% water). No meaningful bulk sugar (<0.3 g/100 g carbohydrate, ~0 free sugar), so the DOMINANT carbon/energy substrates are organic acids (quinic > gallic > oxalic > malic/citric/succinic) plus soluble polyphenols (catechins/theaflavins/thearubigins), and the dominant organic-N source is L-theanine with minor free amino acids; total protein ~0. P
curation_added: quinic acid (dominant organic acid of tea)

curation_removed.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
curation_removed.reason: Synthetic contraceptive-drug steroid; biologically implausible in a plant infusion. FooDB predicted/contaminant noise.

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: Animal-form vitamin A; a plant food (tea) supplies beta-carotene, not preformed retinol. FooDB implausible entry.

curation_removed.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
curation_removed.reason: Apiaceae/citrus furanocoumarin, not a tea constituent; FooDB mis-assignment/predicted, not a growth nutrient.

curation_removed.name: Aluminum / Aluminum
curation_removed.reason: Non-nutritive trace metal; tea accumulates Al but it is not a growth nutrient. Per curation rule drop aluminium.

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: Non-nutritive trace contaminant metal; not a growth nutrient of tea at relevant levels.

curation_removed.name: Co2+ (cobalt ion)
curation_removed.reason: Trace contaminant metal; tea B12/cobalamin is ~0 so cobalt has no growth role here. FooDB noise.

curation_removed.name: Molybdate
curation_removed.reason: Trace element with no measured relevant level in brewed tea; retain only if the model requires a Mo source (borderline noise).

curation_removed.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
curation_removed.reason: Redundant/compartment-artifact iron entries; consolidated into a single 'iron' component (0.02 mg/100 g).

curation_removed.name: Starch C12H20O10
curation_removed.reason: Brewed tea infusion contains essentially no starch; leaf starch does not solubilise. Implausible growth substrate at infusion strength.

curation_removed.name: Chromium
curation_removed.reason: Non-nutritive trace metal with no meaningful measured level in the infusion; FooDB noise.

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
basis: per 100 g brewed infusion (~100 mL), unsweetened black tea prepared from ~1 g leaf / 100 mL tap water
